macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stock Markets Decline Amid Oil Spike and Weak Jobs Report
items.summary: Major U.S. stock indices fell sharply on March 6, 2026, with the S&P 500 down 1.57%, the Dow down 1.9%, and the NASDAQ down 1.58%. The decline was driven by oil prices nearing $90 per barrel amid ongoing Iran tensions and disappointing nonfarm payroll data showing a loss of 92,000 jobs in February.

items.title: U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls Fall 92,000 in February; Unemployment Rises to 4.4%
items.summary: The U.S. labor market weakened in February with nonfarm payrolls declining by 92,000, a sharp reversal from January's gain of 126,000 and well below economist expectations of 50,000 growth. The unemployment rate rose to 4.4% from 4.3%.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge as Iran Conflict Escalates; $150 Oil Possible
items.summary: Oil prices surged near $90 per barrel as tensions with Iran intensify, with no clear end to the conflict in sight. Qatar's energy minister warned that oil could potentially double to over $150, threatening global economic growth.

items.title: Asian Markets Show Mixed Performance; Hong Kong Gains 1.72%
items.summary: Asian stock markets displayed mixed results on March 6, with the Hang Seng Index rising 1.72%, the Nikkei Stock Average up 0.62%, and the Shanghai Composite up 0.38%. However, Australia's S&P/ASX 200 declined 1.00%.

items.title: European Markets Open Mixed; DAX Up 0.53%, FTSE 100 Flat
items.summary: European stock indices opened with mixed signals on March 6, with Germany's DAX Index up 0.53% and France's CAC 40 up 0.47%, while the FTSE 100 remained essentially flat with a 0.00% change.

items.title: Volatility Index Surges to 28.40; Gold and Safe-Haven Assets Rise
items.summary: Market volatility increased significantly with the VIX rising 4.70 points to 28.40, reflecting investor anxiety. Gold prices climbed $17.85 to $5,089.73, while Bitcoin declined $2,113 to $68,751.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits One-Month High of $74K Amid Iran-US Conflict and ETF Inflows
items.summary: Bitcoin reached a one-month high of $74,000 with $1.14 billion in ETF inflows over three days as institutions buy during market chaos from the ongoing Iran-US war. Despite oil prices at $84 and risks of $100 oil crashing markets, BTC shows resilience.

items.title: BTC Consolidates Above $70K After 2% Drop, Eyes $109K or Drop to $101K
items.summary: Bitcoin is consolidating around $70,000-$71,000 following a 2% decline in the last 24 hours, with bullish scenarios targeting over $109,000 and bearish risks below $101,575. Ethereum holds above $1,890 aiming for $2,478.
items.source: WHAT WILL BITCOIN DO TODAY YouTube

items.title: BTC Remains Resilient Despite US-Iran Conflict, Potential Bottom Formation
items.summary: Bitcoin demonstrates bottom firmness amid the US-Iran conflict, with March outlook suggesting consolidation and possible upward trend by early summer. Analysis reviews February declines due to demand, supply, and time lags.
items.source: トウシル [楽天証券] YouTube

items.title: Oil Spike to $100 from Strait of Hormuz Closure Threatens Crypto and Stocks
items.summary: Day 6 of Iran-US war keeps Strait of Hormuz shut, pushing oil to $84 with Goldman Sachs warning of $100 Brent crude in five weeks, spiking inflation and preventing Fed rate cuts. Bitcoin holds steady as a hedge.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: 2026 UK Video Game Awards Winners Announced
items.summary: The UK Interactive Entertainment (Ukie) revealed winners including Battlefield 6 as Game of the Year by Criterion Games, Atomfall as Best UK Console Game by Rebellion, and nDreams as Best UK Developer. The ceremony on March 5 celebrated British talent in AAA, mobile, VR, and more.

items.title: Nvidia CEO Claims Company Created Modern Video Game Industry Amid AI Backlash
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated his company 'created the modern video game industry,' highlighting its GPU role, but faces criticism for AI upscaling like DLSS reducing game optimization efforts.

items.title: Project Helix May Be Microsoft's Final Xbox Hardware Push, Analyst Warns
items.summary: Analyst suggests Project Helix is Microsoft's last chance to salvage its hardware business, emphasizing the need for it to outperform competitors like Steam Deck and ensure broad Xbox game support.

items.title: In-Game Advertising Market to Hit $20.7 Billion by 2030
items.summary: The in-game advertising sector, valued at $11.03 billion in 2025, is forecasted to reach $12.5 billion in 2026 and grow to $20.7 billion by 2030 at a 13.5% CAGR.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: AMD Announces Major 2026 Investments in CPUs, GPUs, and Rack-Scale Systems
items.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su outlined significant 2026 investments in next-generation Instinct MI455X GPUs, EPYC “Venice” CPUs, Pensando “Vulcano” networking, and silicon photonics to meet 100x global compute demand growth. The company is expanding rack-scale AI solutions, open software, and developer tools for performance and efficiency.[1]

items.title: Canada Invests in Advanced Machining and Automation for NextGen Mold Technologies
items.summary: The Government of Canada is funding NextGen Mold Technologies in Windsor, Ontario, to adopt advanced machining technology and automation, enhancing manufacturing efficiency and supporting the automotive sector amid tariffs and trade challenges.[3]
items.source: Government of Canada - Department of Finance

items.title: Liongard CEO Details 2026 Focus on SaaS Discovery and Platform Monetization
items.summary: Liongard CEO Michelle Accardi announced 2026 investments in broader discovery across SaaS, identity, cloud, and network assets, with built-in context, prioritization, and a monetizable platform for partners.[1]

items.title: Event Tech Sector Highlights Major Investment and Partnerships in March Roundup
items.summary: A key investment dominated event tech news, with companies emphasizing partnerships, integrations, and upgrades in March 2026.[4]
